meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing Projects to Build Your Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project ideas. Learn to build SaaS pricing engines using Antigravity and Claude Code to level up your engineering portfolio today.
content.answer_block: Break the cycle of 'tutorial hell' by building a dynamic SaaS pricing engine. By integrating Stripe API with Antigravity, you transform abstract theory into a functional, market-ready tool. This project demonstrates your ability to handle complex billing logic, secure API integrations, and scalable architecture—the exact skills hiring managers demand.
content.intro:
The screen stares back, a blinking cursor in the void of a blank IDE. You have the technical skills, but the spark of a 'real' project remains elusive. You’re trapped in the purgatory of building yet another To-Do list, while the industry screams for engineers who understand the financial backbone of software: SaaS pricing.

Transitioning from student to engineer requires more than just code; it requires building systems that solve business problems. By focusing on the mechanics of subscription tiers, usage-based billing, and checkout flows, you move from writing scripts to architecting professional-grade SaaS infrastructure. This is where your portfolio shifts from 'academic' to 'hireable'.

steps.title: Define the Pricing Model Architecture
steps.description: Before writing code, map your database schema for tiered subscriptions. Understand the relationship between users, plans, and active billing states.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema definition for a SaaS pricing model supporting Free, Pro, and Enterprise tiers with monthly/yearly billing cycles.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Stripe Integration
steps.description: Connect your backend to Stripe's API to handle product creation and checkout session orchestration.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Node.js controller that creates a Stripe Checkout session for a specific subscription plan ID.

steps.title: Implement Webhook Logic
steps.description: Securely handle events from Stripe to update user subscription status in your local database automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Express webhook handler that validates Stripe signatures and updates the user's 'subscription_status' in PostgreSQL.

steps.title: Build the Pricing UI Component
steps.description: Create a responsive, high-conversion pricing table that dynamically switches between monthly and yearly pricing toggles.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a React component using Tailwind CSS for a pricing table with a toggle switch between monthly and annual billing.

steps.title: Add Usage-Based Metering
steps.description: Implement a middleware to track API usage and gate access once a user hits their tier-specific limits.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware function that checks a user's current usage count against their Stripe subscription limit before allowing an API request.

pro_tips: Always use idempotency keys when calling billing APIs to prevent double-charging during network retries.
Never store price values as floats; always use integers in the smallest currency unit (cents) to avoid rounding errors.
Design your database to support 'grandfathered' pricing so you can change plans later without breaking existing users.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your webhook handler to prevent immediate service disruption on failed payments.
Use environment variables for all API keys, and never commit a .env file to your public GitHub repository.
content.next_steps: Deploy your pricing engine to Vercel, integrate a mock dashboard, and write a blog post documenting the technical challenges you solved.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to build a project that actually matters to recruiters. Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea and start shipping the infrastructure that powers the modern web. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
